title: basella alba var. cordifolia
intro: Basella alba var. cordifolia, also known as Indian spinach or Malabar spinach, is a type of leafy green vegetable native to tropical Asia. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial compounds, which are used to treat various health issues such as fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions. The health benefits of consuming Basella alba var. cordifolia include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, making it a valuable addition to a healthy diet. In traditional medicine, the plant is prepared as a decoction, infusion, or paste, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
